title: minuartia cherlerioides subsp. rionii
intro: Minuartia cherlerioides subsp. rionii, commonly known as a subspecies of the lesser pearlwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Minuartia cherlerioides subsp. rionii lie in its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential use in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its application in wound healing and skin care due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, Minuartia cherlerioides subsp. rionii can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion to harness its medicinal properties, and it may also be used in topical applications such as creams and ointments.
